cases.prompt: set up github actions CI for my python package with lint, mypy and pytest across 3.10-3.13
add an all-checks-passed job so I only need one required status check
gh actions workflow for lint + typecheck + tests with uv
cache uv in CI, dependency installs are slow
harden our workflows — pin actions to commit SHAs and lock down GITHUB_TOKEN permissions
run zizmor on our workflow files and fix what it finds
make lint and tests required before anything merges to main
shoud we migrate from branch protection to rulesets?
merge queue says waiting for status checks but the PR ran green — whats wrong
add a python version matrix to the ci workflow
PR stuck on 'waiting for status to be reported' after I renamed the test job
combine coverage from all the matrix legs and upload it once in CI
Could you please configure continuous integration quality gates for this repository? We use ruff, pyright and pytest.
set up the tag-triggered PyPI publish workflow with trusted publishing
automate version bumps and changelog generation when we cut a release
add a pre-commit config with ruff and mypy and make CI run the same hooks
CI is red with a bunch of ruff errors — fix the lint failures
set coverage fail_under to 85 and enable branch coverage in pyproject
set up tox so I can test across python versions locally
enable dependabot updates for our github actions
turn on secret scanning and push protection for this repo
add CodeQL code scanning to the repository
mypy fails in CI with a pile of attr-defined errors, fix them
why did the tests fail on the ubuntu runner? summarize the log from the last run
rerun the failed workflow jobs on PR #42
set up github actions CI for this python package — ruff, mypy, pytest on 3.10 through 3.13
expected_behavior: creates .github/workflows/ci.yml with separate lint, type-check, and test jobs that run the tools the repo already configures, via uv
test job uses a matrix with quoted Python version strings so 3.10 is not parsed as the float 3.1
uses astral-sh/setup-uv with caching enabled and uv sync --locked instead of ad-hoc pip installs
adds an all-checks-passed aggregator job with if: always() that exits non-zero when any needed job failed or was cancelled
sets top-level permissions to contents: read and a concurrency group that cancels superseded PR runs

cases.prompt: make CI checks required so nothing can merge to main when tests fail
expected_behavior: recommends marking only the single aggregator job as required, never individual matrix legs
provides a concrete gh api or ruleset command rather than only UI click-path instructions
warns the check must have run at least once before it appears in the required-checks picker
handles merge queues by ensuring the workflow also triggers on merge_group, or confirms the repo does not use them
warns that a skipped job counts as success, so required jobs must not be silently skippable

cases.prompt: harden our github actions workflows
expected_behavior: pins every third-party action to a full 40-character commit SHA with a version comment, and explains the mutable-tag risk
adds or tightens per-workflow or per-job GITHUB_TOKEN permissions instead of relying on repo-wide write defaults
runs a pinned version of zizmor and triages its findings
flags pull_request_target and Actions-cache use as trust boundaries
stays out of Dependabot, CodeQL, and secret scanning, deferring them to supply-chain work

cases.prompt: our PR is green but the merge queue is stuck waiting for status checks
expected_behavior: diagnoses a missing merge_group trigger as the most likely cause and adds it to the workflow's on: block
checks that the required-check name in branch settings still matches an existing job or check name
explains why matrix or renamed jobs strand required checks and proposes the aggregator job as the durable fix
does not weaken or bypass protection settings just to unblock the merge

cases.prompt: coverage upload from our test matrix produces duplicate reports — fix the workflow
expected_behavior: uploads coverage data per matrix leg as artifacts whose names include the matrix values to avoid collisions
adds a downstream combine job that downloads all legs and produces one combined report
moves any coverage gate to the combine job instead of per-leg enforcement
notes that coverage.py settings such as relative_files belong in the repo's coverage config, not the workflow YAML
